description: db.log_trend() called AFTER engine.on_trend() — if engine raises, trend never persisted
resolution_notes: Refactored: trend_data built first, engine.on_trend() wrapped in try/except, db.log_trend() always called after

description: tracker._flush() clears self._pending even if db.log_price_snapshots_batch() fails — snapshots lost silently
resolution_notes: Copies pending to local list, only clears on success, logs warning on failure

description: Tracked tokens from removed markets never resolve — no price updates = no resolution detection
resolution_notes: Added cleanup_removed_tokens() method, called after market_refresher removes tokens

description: surge_id is None when trend fires without simultaneous surge — trade has no surge FK link
resolution_notes: By design. Trend can fire on accumulated history without a new surge in the current tick. surge_id=NULL is correct — the trend itself is logged in the trends table.

description: Price history before first surge is not captured — tracking only starts when surge/trend fires
resolution_notes: Tracking all 750 tokens would generate 130M rows/day. Only tracking tokens with activity is the right tradeoff.
